message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
This is a dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (do NOT flag as bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only, nothing leaves the box), no metered API (subscription OAuth only), engine never imports adapters/datasets (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends are lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; prefer findings you can back with a concrete repro/PoC against the venv. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects (not style/nits). Each finding needs a real file path and, where possible, a line.

You are independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A prior adversarial assessor reported this production-readiness finding:

TITLE: Failed runs are terminated in the experiment tracker as FINISHED — a failed run is indistinguishable from a successful one in the metric store
DIMENSION: observability & provenance completeness
CLAIMED SEVERITY: MEDIUM
FILE: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py 522-527 (finally) and tracking.py:84-85 (end_run)
DESCRIPTION: run_study's finally block calls tracker.end_run(run_id) on every exit path, including when a phase raised. MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run calls client.set_terminated(run_id) with no status argument, and MLflow's set_terminated defaults status to "FINISHED" (confirmed from the installed mlflow: the signature is set_terminated(run_id, status=None, ...) and the docstring states it "Defaults to FINISHED"). Result: a run that aborted on a firewall violation or a blocked gate is recorded in the on-box experiment store with status FINISHED, identical to a clean completion. Operators querying the tracking store (the canonical place to see which runs succeeded) cannot tell failed runs from successful ones, and partial metrics logged before the failure sit under a run that looks complete. This defeats the tracker's role as a production run-status surface.
SUGGESTED FIX: Distinguish success from failure: capture the outcome in run_study (e.g. set an `ok` flag true only after trail.verify() succeeds) and pass a status into end_run. Add a status parameter to ExperimentTracker.end_run / MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run (default 'FINISHED', pass 'FAILED' on the exception path) so set_terminated(run_id, status=status) marks aborted runs FAILED. The set_terminated call already tolerates the 'KILLED'/'FAILED' RunStatus values.

Independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the actual code; attempt a concrete PoC against the venv if applicable. Set is_real=false if it is a false positive, already-handled, or merely a documented/justified design choice given the hard constraints. Recalibrate severity. Provide your evidence (what you read/ran).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol, runtime_checkable
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	@runtime_checkable
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str) -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        for k, v in dict(params).items():
75	            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
76	        return run_id
77	
78	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
79	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
80	
81	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
82	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
83	
84	    def end_run(self, run_id: str) -> None:
85	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id)

content.text: The code confirms the finding. `end_run` calls `set_terminated(run_id)` with no status, and the `finally` block calls it on every exit path. Let me verify MLflow's default behavior in the venv and check if there's any other status-setting path.

content.text: Confirmed: `set_terminated` defaults to "FINISHED". Now let me do a concrete PoC: simulate a failed run and check the recorded status in the store.

cd /tmp && rm -f poc_assay.db && ASSAY_TRACKING_URI="sqlite:////tmp/poc_assay.db" /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python -c "
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src')
from assay_engine.observability.tracking import MlflowExperimentTracker
from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient

t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
# Simulate a run that aborts mid-way (like a FirewallViolation/GateError)
rid = t.start_run('failed-study', {'modes': 'x'})
t.log_metric(rid, 'partial_metric', 1.0)
# pipeline.py finally block does exactly this on the failure path:
t.end_run(rid)

c = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=t._uri)
run = c.get_run(rid)
print('status of an ABORTED run as recorded:', run.info.status)
print('partial metric still present:', run.data.metrics)

The PoC confirms the finding empirically: an aborted run is recorded as FINISHED with partial metrics present.

Let me verify the failure paths actually do hit the `finally` block (i.e., that phases can raise) and confirm there's no other status reconciliation elsewhere.

content.text:
Confirmed. There are many `raise` paths inside the `try` (FirewallViolation, GateError, IngestionError, ValueError, and `trail.verify()` failures), all of which hit the `finally` that calls `end_run` → `set_terminated` with default FINISHED. There is no FAILED status handling anywhere in the codebase.

My assessment:

The finding is real and accurate. The code and PoC both confirm it. Let me calibrate severity. This is on-box observability/provenance for a production engine. It is not a correctness bug in the ML pipeline itself (the exception still propagates to the caller, so a programmatic caller knows the run failed via the exception). The defect is specifically in the *tracking store* surface: aborted runs are indistinguishable from clean completions when queried later, and partial metrics sit under a FINISHED-looking run. This matters for production observability/audit but is a degraded-signal issue, not data loss or a security/correctness failure. MEDIUM is the right calibration (arguably LOW-MEDIUM since the tracker is optional and the exception path is otherwise preserved, but for a system whose stated value is provenance/observability completeness, MEDIUM holds).

input.reasoning: Verified true and reproduced. In /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py the run_study body wraps every phase in a try whose finally block (lines 522-527) unconditionally calls tracker.end_run(run_id) on all exit paths, including the many in-try raises (FirewallViolation at 299/312/317/338/363/375/379/412/517, GateError at 246, IngestionError at 277/279, ValueError at 435, and a failed trail.verify() at 521). MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run (observability/tracking.py:84-85) calls self._client().set_terminated(run_id) with no status argument. I confirmed against the installed venv that MlflowClient.set_terminated has signature (run_id, status=None, end_time=None) and its docstring states status "Defaults to 'FINISHED'". I ran a concrete PoC against the venv (ASSAY_TRACKING_URI sqlite store): started a run, logged a partial metric, called end_run on the abort path exactly as the finally does, then read it back — recorded status was FINISHED and the partial metric was still present. I grepped the entire src tree: there is no FAILED/KILLED/RunStatus handling anywhere, so no other code reconciles the status. Net effect: an aborted run (firewall breach, blocked gate, broken provenance chain) is stored in the on-box experiment tracker as FINISHED, identical to a clean completion, with partial metrics underneath. This is a genuine observability/provenance-completeness defect for a system whose stated value is provenance, and is not excused by any of the hard constraints (it is a local-store status, unrelated to networking/adapters/lazy-imports). It is degraded-signal rather than correctness/data-loss: the exception still propagates to the caller, the tracker is optional (best-effort), and the run record plus its params/partial metrics are retained — only the terminal status label is wrong. Hence MEDIUM, matching the claimed severity. The suggested fix (thread an ok flag set true only after trail.verify, add a status param to end_run defaulting FINISHED and pass FAILED on the exception path) is sound; set_terminated accepts FAILED/KILLED RunStatus values.
input.evidence: Read pipeline.py lines 240-269 (start_run) and 480-539 (try/finally + post-finally StudyResult); read observability/tracking.py in full (end_run at 84-85 calls set_terminated with no status). Venv introspection: MlflowClient.set_terminated signature (self, run_id, status: str|None=None, end_time: int|None=None) and docstring 'Defaults to "FINISHED"'. PoC (/tmp, sqlite store via ASSAY_TRACKING_URI) output: "status of an ABORTED run as recorded: FINISHED" and "partial metric still present: {'partial_metric': 1.0}". grep across src/ for set_terminated/FAILED/RunStatus/FINISHED found only the default-status call site and the protocol/impl of end_run — no FAILED handling exists. grep of pipeline.py confirms numerous raise statements inside the guarded try, all routing through the finally.

content.text: Verified the finding is real. An aborted run (firewall violation, blocked gate, broken provenance chain) is recorded in the on-box MLflow store as FINISHED — indistinguishable from a clean completion — because `end_run` in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py:84-85` calls `set_terminated(run_id)` with no status, and the `finally` block in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:522-527` runs it on every exit path. I confirmed MLflow's default-FINISHED behavior in the venv and reproduced the wrong status (with partial metrics still attached) via a direct PoC. Severity confirmed as MEDIUM.
